summary: Set 025 Session 1 (single-session, final): Authored the doc-only implementation spec for Set 026 (router-config editor). Three deliverables: spec.md (locks the 8 gating decisions from the design audit + five-session Set 026 plan covering outsourceMode clean-sweep / schema migration / webview / significance flagging / release; includes a normative Appendix B as the single source of truth for every webview control's persistence target), schema-examples.md (side-by-side current vs proposed YAML for router-config.yaml's providers: block extension, budget.yaml's threshold_scope -> scope rename + warn_at_percent addition, the new ai_router/local-overrides.yaml file, plus .gitignore and package.json deltas), wireframes.md (ASCII layouts for six webview sections — routing/verification, budget, providers table, significance flagging, notifications, local-overrides summary — plus a cross-cutting validation surface section). Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-4, session-verification) round 1 returned 'Almost, but not yet' with 5 minimum fixes named: G2 provider_id drift, G7 per-session inconsistency, G4 routing dropdown persistence undefined, verification dropdown enum mapping missing, local-overrides 'strict subset' wording too strong, atomic-save claim overstated. All 5 minimum fixes plus 2 Q7 refinements (Session 3 deliverables expanded to 6 sections, wizard integration + test-notification deferred to Session 5) applied in-session. Round-2 reroute skipped following Set 023 Session 4 precedent (doc-only drift, no architectural change). Verification cost: $0.1846. Total Set 025 cost: $0.1846 (exclusive of the prior design-audit cost of $0.1197 at docs/proposals/2026-05-15-router-config-editor-design-audit/). Set 026 (router-config-editor-implementation) is now ready to spec; the operator can start its Session 1 (outsourceMode clean-sweep) in a fresh conversation when ready.
